# @zakkster/lite-headless / stepper

Headless numeric stepper (W3C `role="spinbutton"`). A single `value`
signal drives both an input element (parsed on commit) and one or more
increment/decrement controls. The display is locale-aware via
`Intl.NumberFormat` by default; consumers can also pass a custom
formatter/parser pair for currency, percentages, time-of-day, etc.

Hot-path discipline: arrow keys and button clicks go through one
`publish()` per interaction; `_isTyping` guards the reactive sync so
button-driven updates don't clobber user typing. The default parser
derives decimal/group separators ONCE from the formatter at
construction (no per-keystroke `formatToParts`).

## API

createStepper({
    value?:        Signal<number>,            // controlled mode
    defaultValue?: number,
    min?:          number,                    // default -Infinity
    max?:          number,                    // default  Infinity
    step?:         number,                    // default 1
    largeStep?:    number,                    // default 10 (shift+arrow, PageUp/Down)
    precision?:    number,                    // default Intl formatting fraction digits
    formatter?:    (n) => string,             // custom formatter
    parser?:       (str) => number,           // custom parser
    locale?:       string,                    // for default Intl.NumberFormat
    disabled?:     boolean,                   // initial disabled state
    selectOnFocus?: boolean,
    repeatDelay?:    number,                  // default 400ms
    repeatInterval?: number,                  // default 50ms
    onValueChange?: (value, reason) => void,
})

  attachInput(el)            // role=spinbutton, takes keystrokes
  attachIncrement(el)        // any element; click + pointerdown + Enter/Space + auto-repeat
  attachDecrement(el)
  attachReadout(el)          // display-only; textContent reactively updates

  value()                    // raw number
  displayValue()             // formatted string
  setValue(n, reason?)
  increment(amount?)         // default = step
  decrement(amount?)
  setDisabled(flag)
  destroy()

## Keyboard contract (on the input)

  ArrowUp   / ArrowDown    -> +/- step
  shift + ArrowUp/Down     -> +/- largeStep
  PageUp    / PageDown     -> +/- largeStep
  Home      / End          -> min / max (if finite)
  Enter                    -> commit current text

Increment/decrement controls also activate on Enter and Space, with
pointer-hold auto-repeat after `repeatDelay`.

## Locale-aware formatting

Default formatter:

  new Intl.NumberFormat(locale, {
      minimumFractionDigits: precision,
      maximumFractionDigits: precision,
      useGrouping: false,
  });

Default parser derives separators from `formatToParts` once at
construction:

  de-DE -> decimal "," group "."    -> "3,14"      parses to 3.14
  en-US -> decimal "." group ","    -> "1,000.5"   parses to 1000.5

If you pass a custom formatter (currency, percent, etc.) supply a
matching parser -- the default parser only handles plain numeric text.

## Custom element

<lite-stepper min="0" max="100" step="1" value="50" large-step="10">
  <button data-decrement>−</button>
  <input data-input>
  <button data-increment>+</button>
</lite-stepper>

Or display-only:

<lite-stepper min="0" max="9" step="1" value="3">
  <button data-decrement>−</button>
  <output data-readout></output>
  <button data-increment>+</button>
</lite-stepper>

Attributes: `min`, `max`, `step`, `large-step`, `precision`, `locale`,
`value`, `disabled`, `select-on-focus`. The `value` and `disabled`
attributes are reactively synced via `useAttr`.

Dispatches `CustomEvent('valuechange', { detail: { value, reason } })`.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
